content: The Jets have agreed to trade quarterback Justin Fields to the Chiefs, pending a physical, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Tide's No. 2 scorer arrested, facing felony charges
content: Alabama's Aden Holloway was arrested, released from jail on bond and will face felony marijuana charges. The Crimson Tide open the NCAA tournament vs. Hofstra on Friday.

content: Oregon quarterback Dante Moore has revealed he struggled with mental health issues associated with the pressures of major college football.

content: The 49ers and wide receiver Christian Kirk have agreed to a one-year contract, a source confirmed to ESPN.

title: WNBPA, WNBA confirm progress amid CBA talks
content: WNBPA executive director Terri Carmichael Jackson said that while progress is being made in CBA negotiations, a delay to the 2026 season could not be ruled out.

title: Italy changes plan, to start Nola vs. Venezuela
content: Aaron Nola will start Monday night's World Baseball Classic semifinal for Italy against Venezuela rather than Michael Lorenzen.

content: Ex-UFC star Nate Diaz, 40, will compete in an MMA fight for the first time in four years when he takes on Mike Perry as part of Most Valuable Promotions' MMA debut on May 16.

content: LAFC have already made history with their lockdown defense, but are they No. 1? Here's this week's edition of ESPN's MLS Power Rankings.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal want to keep Calafiori despite Serie A interest
content: Inter Milan, Juventus and other Italian clubs want Riccardo Calafiori, but Arsenal intend to retain him. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Racing around Cowboys', Rangers' stadiums was just what IndyCar needed
content: Racing around the homes of the Cowboys and Rangers made IndyCar's Grand Prix of Arlington the signature event that the sport has been desperate for.

title: RS elections: NDA sweeps Bihar, bags three in Odisha; Haryana result put on hold
content: The NDA has significantly bolstered its Rajya Sabha presence, securing all five seats in Bihar and three of four in Odisha. Haryana's results are pending due to a vote secrecy controversy involving Congress MLAs. This round of elections saw the NDA win eight of eleven contested seats, strengthening its position in the Upper House.

title: 'Distorted picture of India': MEA slams US report recommending sanctions on RSS, R&AW
content: India has strongly rejected the US Commission on International Religious Freedom's 2026 report, calling it biased and distorted. The MEA spokesperson criticized the commission's reliance on questionable sources and ideological narratives, urging it to address religious freedom issues within the US. India maintains its commitment to religious harmony.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance built on a single chip for all AI tasks is facing a challenge.  As inference, the running of AI models, becomes more crucial and cost-sensitive, customers are exploring cheaper, purpose-built alternatives from rivals like Google and Meta.  Jensen Huang's expected unveiling of a new inference-focused chip signals a potential shift away from Nvidia's long-held strategy.

title: England all-rounder blasts Brendon McCullum, reveals 'an eye-opening experience'
content: Liam Livingstone criticised England management, including coach Brendon McCullum and director Rob Key, for poor communication after he was dropped in 2025. The all-rounder said the experience was “eye-opening” and expressed frustration over his role and batting position. Now with Sunrisers Hyderabad for the Indian Premier League 2026, he hopes strong performances revive his England chances.

title: FBI is investigating malware-laden games on Steam: Names; timeline of attack and tips
content: The FBI is investigating malware embedded in games on Steam, with a focus on potential victims between May 2024 and January 2026. Several games, including BlockBlasters and Chemia, have been identified as containing the malicious software. The agency is urging anyone who may have installed these games or has relevant information to come forward.

title: 'Fail fast.Succeed sooner': Amazon devices and services head's advice for techies
content: Amazon Devices chief Panos Panay urged engineers to embrace failure, calling it a crucial step for innovation.  He shared this 'hard-earned learning' with India teams, emphasizing that learning from mistakes accelerates success.  Panay highlighted the passion and AI advancements seen during his visit, underscoring India's vital role in building future products.

title: $3.4 trillion mistake with China: Why US will make India's rise far tougher
content: The US has spent a staggering $3.4 trillion on a "militarised rivalry" with China since 2012, a figure dwarfing its Afghanistan war costs. This massive peacetime spending, driven by a strategic pivot to Asia, highlights Washington's determination to avoid repeating past "mistakes" with Beijing, particularly concerning technological transfers and economic engagement, as it now recalibrates its approach with India.

title: Radko Gudas hit ends Auston Matthews’ season, sparks Crosby frustration
content: The NHL handed Radko Gudas a five-game suspension after a kneeing play left Auston Matthews with a season-ending MCL tear. The incident also revived anger tied to Sidney Crosby’s earlier knee injury, which forced him to miss Canada’s Olympic gold medal game. Both the Pittsburgh Penguins and Toronto Maple Leafs now face major lineup gaps, and many fans believe the punishment does little to reflect the long-term damage caused by the collisions.

title: Maria Sharapova and Luka Doncic's photo goes viral after Lakers’ OT win
content: Tennis icon Maria Sharapova made a surprise appearance at an NBA game and later posed for a photo with Luka Dončić after the Lakers’ thrilling overtime win. The photo quickly went viral online.

title: Fans mock Travis Kelce after he credits Taylor Swift for NFL comeback
content: Travis Kelce revealed that fiancée Taylor Swift motivated him to continue his NFL career, praising her creativity and passion. His comments quickly went viral, sparking mixed fan reactions online. While some admired the couple’s supportive dynamic, others responded with sarcasm and criticism, questioning his motives and joking about Swift influencing his decision to keep playing.

title: The invisible arc of power: How ballistic missiles reshaped modern warfare
content: Ballistic missiles, born from wartime innovation, revolutionized warfare by making distance irrelevant. These rocket-powered weapons, capable of delivering payloads across continents in minutes, evolved into the backbone of modern strategic deterrence. Understanding their history, function, and global proliferation is crucial to grasping today's security environment.

title: Trump says Iran has been ‘literally obliterated’ as he presses allies to help reopen Hormuz
content: US President Trump declared Iran's military "literally obliterated" after over 7,000 strikes, claiming significant reductions in missile and drone attacks. Despite asserting control over the Strait of Hormuz, he urged allies to assist in securing shipping, but faced cool responses from key nations like Germany and Japan.

title: 'Hard to argue': Ponting reveals ‘overlooked’ factor in India’s T20 WC success
content: Ricky Ponting hailed the India national cricket team as one of the strongest T20 sides after their triumph in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, where they beat the New Zealand national cricket team by 96 runs at Narendra Modi Stadium. Ponting credited India’s depth, experience and Indian Premier League exposure for their dominance in white-ball cricket and back-to-back titles.

title: IPL 2026: Injured Harshit Rana on crutches at BCCI Naman Awards - Watch
content: Harshit Rana was seen on crutches at the BCCI Naman Awards, indicating his recovery is not progressing as expected. The Kolkata Knight Riders fast bowler is set to miss the IPL 2026 season due to a knee injury sustained earlier this year. This setback adds to KKR's bowling concerns for the upcoming tournament.

title: Laughter Chefs fame Jannat Zubair, brother Ayaan allegedly attacked on highway
content: In a harrowing turn of events, popular influencers Jannat Zubair and her brother Ayaan experienced a frightening road incident in Panvel, characterized by a chase and an aggressive encounter. Fortunately, both siblings are reported to be unharmed and are currently cooperating with law enforcement as they investigate the incident.

title: 'Cannot be expressed': Sanju Samson makes massive Gambhir admission
content: Sanju Samson reflected on India’s victory in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, where he was Player of the Tournament with 321 runs. He spoke about his long bond with head coach Gautam Gambhir and revealed receiving an emotional message from Sachin Tendulkar after India defeated the New Zealand national cricket team in the final.

title: Car hits cop, carries him on bonnet for 4km near Hosur
content: The arrested man has been identified as Yarap Bhasha, 34, the Kalekunda of Karnataka. The incident happened on Sunday evening when a team from the Hudco police station was checking vehicles.

title: Horoscope: Daily astrological predictions for March 17
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for March 17, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Breathless after climbing stairs? Cardiologist explains heart warning signs
content: Taking the stairs could be a straightforward way to assess your heart health. Experts emphasize that your breathing and heart rate's recovery time post-exercise reflects your cardiovascular condition. If you find it takes longer than expected to bounce back, it might be a sign of stiff arteries or heart issues, highlighting the need for medical evaluation to safeguard your health.

title: YouTuber Markiplier’s Oscars red carpet absence sparks online controversy
content: Popular YouTuber Markiplier's absence from the 2026 Oscars red carpet sparked online buzz. He later revealed a backstage mix-up with VIP staff led him astray, causing him to miss the walk. Despite the confusion, his attendance signifies the growing influence of online creators in Hollywood, a point fans found noteworthy.

title: ‘Floorboard vibrations, noise from belly section’: AI New York-Delhi flight diverted
content: Air India's New York to Delhi flight made an unexpected landing in Shannon, Ireland, due to passenger-reported vibrations and noise.  The Airbus A350, carrying 300 people, diverted as a safety precaution. Passengers were accommodated in hotels while engineers assess the aircraft, with Air India prioritizing their onward journey.

title: IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan likely to lead SRH
content: Ishan Kishan is the leading candidate to captain Sunrisers Hyderabad in the Indian Premier League 2026 if Pat Cummins is unavailable at the start. Kishan enters the season in strong form after scoring 354 runs in IPL 2025 and 317 runs for the India national cricket team in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, where he helped India win the title. Abhishek Sharma will be Ishan's deputy.

title: Dhoni next India coach? Gambhir's role reversal wish after Instagram exchange
content: Speaking about the exchange a week after the final, Gambhir said he appreciated Dhoni's message and hoped to return the gesture one day.

title: Celina Jaitly reacts as Vikrant Jaitly consults wife first; court dismisses plea
content: Celina Jaitly has reacted after the Delhi High Court dismissed her petition seeking consular access, legal assistance and communication with her brother, Major (Retd.) Vikrant Jaitly, who is currently detained in the United Arab Emirates. The court disposed of the plea after being informed that Vikrant Jaitly did not wish to communicate with his sister and preferred to handle legal matters only after consulting his wife, Charul Jaitly.

title: Chiefs acquire Justin Fields from Jets for 2027 sixth-round pick
content: The Chiefs traded a 2027 sixth-round pick for Justin Fields as Patrick Mahomes rehabs a torn ACL. The Jets paid most of Fields’ 2026 salary to move on after one rough season, making this a low-risk Kansas City swing with real short-term stakes for Travis Kelce and the rest of the offense.

title: Medico suicide case: 5 AIIMS Rajkot students arrested; 17-page note alleges assault by batchmates
content: Five final-year MBBS students from AIIMS Rajkot were arrested for abetting the suicide of a classmate, Ratan Kumar Meghwal. Meghwal died by train on March 14, leaving a 17-page note detailing alleged physical assault and mental harassment by the accused since January.

title: Happy Chaitra Navratri 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes for loved ones
content: Chaitra Navratri, a significant Hindu festival marking the lunar New Year, commences in 2026, dedicated to Goddess Durga and her nine forms. Devotees observe fasts, offer prayers, and seek blessings for prosperity and happiness. The nine-day celebration symbolizes the triumph of good over evil, fostering faith and positivity. Families and friends exchange heartfelt wishes, strengthening bonds during this auspicious period.

title: Hema Malini reacts to Dharmendra Oscars snub claim: Was he in In Memoriam?
content: Indian fans expressed disappointment as veteran actor Dharmendra was seemingly overlooked in the Oscars 2026 'In Memoriam' segment. While his name appeared on the Oscars website, the live broadcast omitted him, sparking online debate. Hema Malini voiced her dismay, calling it a 'shame' for the Academy to ignore such a globally recognized star.

title: 'I’ve spoken to Mahi bhai': Samson breaks silence on 1st time with Dhoni
content: Sanju Samson said he is excited to play alongside MS Dhoni for the first time in the Indian Premier League, calling it a big learning opportunity. The wicketkeeper-batter also reflected on India’s triumph in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, where he was Player of the Tournament. Meanwhile, Mohammed Siraj called his unexpected inclusion in the title-winning squad “a miracle.”

title: Assam polls: BJP keeps tribal council-based ally in limbo
content: The BJP is yet to clear the air about the status of the United People’s Party Liberal in the National Democratic Alliance

title: BJP romps home with three Rajya Sabha seats in Odisha, BJD content with one seat
content: As many as 11 MLAs cross-voted in favour of BJP-backed independent candidate Dilip Ray, who initially did not have numerical strength for winning the Rajya Sabha seat

title: SC declines to intervene in Calcutta High Court order for NIA probe into Beldanga violence
content: On February 11, the apex court questioned the invocation of the UAPA; on March 16,the court finds the High Court’s confirmation of the NIA probe into the case ‘quite balanced’

title: All five NDA candidates win Rajya Sabha polls in Bihar, including CM Nitish Kumar and Nitin Nabin
content: Three MLAs of Congress party and one MLA of Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) skipped the voting. RJD MLA Kumar Sarvjeet alleged that BJP used the money power to win the election.

content: Mr. Sapkal further said that he expressed condolences over the demise of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Hosseini Khamenei

title: Congress sees BJP leader’s bid to trigger polarisation
content: BJP MP from Aligarh, Satish Gautam, had claimed that Muslims were taking the most advantage of welfare schemes

title: Transgender communities across India demand withdrawal of Bill to redefine trans people
content: The Centre’s action led to protests and public meetings within the transgender community across cities such as New Delhi, Pune, Hyderabad and Kolkata, calling for protecting the right to self-perceived gender identity

title: AAP govt. fulfilled all pre-poll promises in just 4 years: Punjab CM
content: Mann says key promises include free electricity, 65,000 govt. jobs and 5.5 lakh employment opportunities; Oppn. calls it ‘most disappointing govt.’

title: BJP announces first list of candidates for 144 seats for Bengal Assembly polls

content: Though the UDF is yet to officially announce its candidates, Congress probables who have received the green signal from top leadership have already begun campaigning. LDF candidates too have entered campaign mode

title: NDA wins eight Rajya Sabha seats from Bihar, Odisha; Haryana results on hold

title: Thoothukudi schoolgirl’s murder: HC directs Vilathikulam DSP to take up probe
content: A Division Bench of Justices N. Sathish Kumar and M. Jothiraman directed the Vilathikulam DSP to record audio-video statements of the parents of the girl, and asked the parents to cooperate with the investigation

title: Hardeep Puri’s daughter moves Delhi HC to remove content linking her to Jeffrey Epstein
content: In her lawsuit seeking ₹10 crore in damages, Himayani Puri has also called for an order to restrain several entities from disseminating defamatory content; it is likely to be heard in the HC next week

title: Congress, DMK hold seat-sharing talks for the second time
content: During the first round of talks held on Saturday last both parties stuck to their stand of leading the INDIA bloc in the U.T. for the 2026 elections

title: Supreme Court says stakeholder consultation needed, reluctant to alter duration of five-year law courses
content: While acknowledging that reforms in legal education are a necessity, the Chief Justice-led Bench says it may not be appropriate for courts to determine the duration of professional courses.

title: Address attacks on Hindu temples in the U.S., MEA says
content: The U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom is ‘presenting a distorted and selective picture of India’, the Ministry of External Affairs says

title: Naval warships escort Indian vessels from the Gulf of Oman
content: Three warships escort Shivalik, Nanda Devi, and Jag Laadki; naval protection for Indian ships to continue; warships operating under Operation Sankalp, a mission aimed at safeguarding merchant shipping and protecting India’s maritime interests in the Gulf region

title: M.P. High Court Bench to visit Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque site before April 2 hearing
content: Under an agreement with the ASI in 2003, Hindus are allowed to perform puja at the complex every Tuesday, while Muslims offer namaz there every Friday.

title: ED arrests Madhya Pradesh food company owner for money laundering, selling adulterated dairy products
content: The company allegedly supplied adulterated dairy products by substituting milk fat with palm oil and other harmful chemicals

title: Five convicted in 2016 Una flogging case; Gujarat court to announce punishment
content: In the July 11, 2016 incident, more than 40 men from the upper caste Darbar community attacked four members of the Sarvaiya family in Mota Samadhiyala village near Una

title: Government clears 23 institutions to set up ‘quantum labs’
content: National Quantum Mission aims to develop 50–1,000 qubit quantum computers, satellite-based secure communication, and high-precision quantum sensors/materials

title: Fuel crisis is man-made, Opposition says, targeting Centre’s foreign policy
content: Participating in the discussion on the Appropriation Bill, senior Congress MP Shaktisinh Gohil says India has been committed to remaining non-aligned since Independence

title: Cabinet berth only for those who ensure victory in maximum number of constituencies, says Stalin
content: Personal differences should not be allowed to affect the party’s prospects in the April 23 Assembly election, CM tells DMK district secretaries

title: Kerala wellness sector stares at uncertainty as West Asian crisis continues
content: Kerala is considered the cradle of wellness tourism in the country, and nearly 70% of the foreign tourists visiting the State belong to this category

title: Amid Death Rumours, Netanyahu's Message For "Brave People Of Iran"
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sent wishes to Iranians Monday for the upcoming Nowruz holidays, marking the Persian new year, as his country presses its war on Iran.

title: Around 200 US Troops Injured In 7 Countries During Iran War
content: Around 200 US military personnel have been Injured in seven countries across the Middle East since the start of the US-Israel war against Iran, a US military spokesman said Monday.

title: Iran, US Have Been In Direct Contact In Recent Days: Reports
content: A direct communications channel between US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has been reactivated in recent days, Axios reported on Monday, citing a US official and a source with knowledge of the matter.

title: Trump Seeks To Reopen Strait Of Hormuz Amid Iran War
content: US President Donald Trump said he has demanded about seven countries send warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as Iranian strikes kept slamming into Gulf countries Monday.

title: 'Arbitrary, Unilateral Move': Mamata Banerjee To Poll Chief Over Reshuffle
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has now written to Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, putting on record her protest against the state's removal of top bureaucrats ahead of the assembly election.

title: RJD Rank And File Turns Against Congress After Rajya Sabha Poll Defeat
content: Rajya Sabha election for one seat in Bihar led to a victory for the NDA after three Congress MLAs and their colleague from the Rashtriya Janata Dal skipped the voting.

title: Netanyahu's Video Proof Of Being Alive Hits A "Disappearing" Ring Problem
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has posted a new video on his official X account in which a ring on his finger appears to vanish as he moves his arm.

title: Video: Cars Abandoned, Tourists Stranded Near Atal Tunnel Amid Snowfall
content: More than 1,000 vehicles have been stranded near the Atal Tunnel outside Manali since Sunday night, after heavy snowfall and ice formation on roads brought traffic to a complete halt for the second consecutive day.

title: Trump Pushes For 'Enthusiasm' From US Allies To Secure Strait of Hormuz
content: President Donald Trump criticized US allies on Monday for their lukewarm response to his call to help protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz during the US war against Iran.

title: DMK Expands Alliance Net As It Eyes Rare Second Term Amid Vijay Factor
content: As the battle for the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections intensifies, the ruling M K Stalin-led Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) is banking heavily on an expansive alliance strategy.

title: Iran War Live Updates: We Destroyed Everything On Kharg Island, Says Trump
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: Centre Trashes Reports Linking Release Of Tankers To Hormuz Access: Report
content: The government on Monday trashed reports that said Tehran is seeking release of three oil tankers seized by India in return for ensuring safe passage to Indian-'flagged vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Haryana Rajya Sabha Polls: Voting Secrecy Row Delays Counting
content: A controversy over the secrecy of votes has delayed the counting process in the Haryana Rajya Sabha Election, with complaints filed by both the Congress and the BJ against each other's MLAs.

title: Trump Says He Predicted 9/11 A Year Before It Happened: Here's The Truth
content: US President Donald Trump on Monday said he had warned about the September 11 terror attacks more than a year before they took place.

title: Tricycle Given By Minister Breaks Down, A Replacement Follows Within Hours
content: A tricycle allegedly broke down minutes after being handed over to a person with disabilities in an incident that occurred during Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia's four-day visit to Guna in Madhya Pradesh.

title: "In The Name Of God": Khamenei's Aide's Letter To "Muslims Of The World"
content: Ali Larijani, the Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, has written a letter to "Muslims of the world" in what was seen as part rebuke, part theological indictment, and part strategic appeal.

title: Trinamool Congress To Contest Assam Polls Independently
content: With the announcement of elections in Assam and several other states, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) has said it will largely contest the upcoming polls in Assam independently.

title: Where Is Mojtaba Khamenei? Iran Minister Shares Big Update With NDTV
content: As the war between Iran, the United States and Israel enters its third week, questions have continued to circulate about the status of Iran's Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei.

title: Fareed Zakaria On 2 Lessons China Took Home In Seeing US Go To War Again
content: The images from the war in the Middle East are being watched carefully in Beijing not with alarm, but with calculation.

title: Joint Tax Filing To Exemption: Raghav Chadha Proposes "Practical Reforms"
content: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) Rajya Sabha member Raghav Chadha on Monday raised what he described as three "everyday anxieties" faced by ordinary Indians and urged the government to introduce constructive reforms.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: Consensus reached to revoke suspension of eight Opposition MPs
content: Sources say parties agree that members will not cross over into the Well of the House to confront those on the other side, throw papers at the Speaker’s podium or protest at the desk of the secretary general.

title: Deve Gowda writes to Sonia Gandhi over ‘chaos’ in Parliament
content: The former Prime Minister raised questions on the conduct of the Leader of the Opposition in leading protests that have disrupted proceedings during the ongoing Budget Session.

title: Trinamool MPs walk out of Rajya Sabha protesting removal of officials
content: Trinamool Congress Rajya Sabha Member Derek O’Brien said the Election Commission removed West Bengal’s top bureaucrats in the “dead of night”

title: Stranded in Doha, Chennai resident reaches Riyadh by road to fly back home
content: Varun Krishnan, who departed for Barcelona on February 28 and returned home on March 7, says the episode has changed the way he views travel — what once seemed like a predictable system of schedules and connections now appears far more uncertain and fragile

title: In Brussels, Jaishankar discusses Iran with EU counterparts
content: The External Affairs Minister was invited to interact with Foreign Ministers from the 27-member bloc, during the EU Foreign Affairs Council meeting, says a statement from Ministry of External Affairs

title: Chennai Super Kings insists Sun TV should not interfere in efforts to obtain licence for using Jailer, Coolie songs in its promos
content: CSKCL makes the statement in an affidavit filed before Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy of Madras High Court on Monday

title: Opposition notice seeking CEC’s removal cites his public remarks against Rahul Gandhi
content: The 10-page notice filed in both Houses also refers to concerns over the process of CEC appointment, and an alleged manipulation of voter rolls in Karnataka

title: 1.12 lakh acres donated to Waqf Board, but it currently controls only 20,054 acres: Zameer
content: The Board lost 23,627 acres following implementation of the land reforms, says Housing, Waqf and Minority Welfare Minister B.Z. Zameer Ahmed Khan; he reiterates that Hindu temples have not encroached upon any Waqf Board properties.

title: Pension issue of aided educational institutions’ staff  will be referred to committee: Madhu Bangarappa
content: BJP member S.V. Sankanur urged the government to consider pension benefits for employees of aided educational institutions who were appointed prior to 2006 and later brought under the grant-in-aid system, as well as those appointed after 2006.

title: Maternity, childcare leave for women teachers to be sanctioned within 15 days
content: Women teachers returning to duty after maternity leave can seek a transfer to a place of their choice during counselling, says School Education and Literacy Minister Madhu Bangarappa; this facility will be available until the child completes five years.

title: SSC exams begin in Andhra Pradesh, 98.90% attendance recorded on Day 1
content: A total of 1,753 centre visits were conducted by monitoring authorities, including District Collectors, District Level Observers, District Educational Officers, ACGEs, District Monitoring Coordinators, Flying Squads, and other officials across examination centres throughout the State

title: GHMC urges property tax defaulters to avail OTS scheme
content: With less than two weeks left for the financial year to end, tax collection in the tri-corporation CURE region has reached ₹2,186 crore against a net demand of ₹6,387 crore

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: In rapidly urbanising Kozhikode North, a closer electoral contest looms
content: Home to several key administrative institutions and educational establishments, the constituency reflects the city’s evolving urban character and complex electoral dynamics. It comprises 32 divisions of the Kozhikode Corporation and is characterised by a sizeable upper- and middle-class voter base.

title: State universities suffering from ‘serious chronic ailments’, need long-term treatment: M.C. Sudhakar
content: Raising the issue under Rule 330, BJP member Talwar Sabanna said State universities had become centres of problems, alleging that they were plagued by caste-based issues and corruption and that their overall functioning had deteriorated.

title: Andhra Pradesh poised to emerge as east-west freight corridor hub: CM
content: Naidu asks officials to fast track Ramayapatnam, Machilipatnam and Mulapeta ports and leverage disruptions in West Asia trade routes to position the State as a major logistics hub

title: B.Ed admission fee revision to be taken up from this academic year
content: Higher Education Minister M.C. Sudhakar said that the admission fee for B.Ed. colleges in the State had not been revised for the past 26 years.

title: As 10,796 remain untraced, Karnataka High Court directs police to effectively implement SOP for handling complaints of missing persons
content: The court has now specifically asked the State government to file a report on establishment of district missing persons units, quarterly review report of supervisory committees, and digital portal for tracking of missing persons, among others

title: New paramedical admission norms may trigger educational crisis, closure of colleges: Sharan Prakash Patil
content: Medical Education Minister Sharan Prakash Patil said the Commission had stipulated that only students who have passed II PUC in the science stream would be eligible for admission to paramedical courses. The rule has created apprehension among institutions in the State.

title: 2.38 lakh students download class 10 hall tickets via WhatsApp service
content: 7,53,834 students accessed their hall tickets through the official website and 65,477 downloaded from the Education Department’s LEAP app

title: ED attaches assets worth ₹50.80 crore in Salai Group case
content: The ED’s probe is based on an FIR registered against Yambem Biren and and Narengbam Samarjit, the self-styled ‘External Affairs & Defence Minister of Manipur State Council’

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: MLA C.C. Mukundan, ousted from CPI, joins BJP; may contest from Nattika
content: Mukundan had earlier held discussions with the State Congress leaders in Delhi, but they refused to accommodate him. He says he will expose instances of corruption in the CPI

content: YSRCP leaders pay tributes on freedom fighter’s 125th birth anniversary, recall his historic 58-day fast-unto-death for a separate Andhra State

title: Jharkhand man chokes on rasgulla, dies
content:
.

JAMSHEPUR: A 41-year-old man died after choking on a rasgulla at a wedding in Jharkhand’s Jamshedpur. Doctors said the syrupy sweet blocked Lalit Singh’s airways, which disrupted oxygen supply and led to his death. Lalit had difficulties in breathing after the rasgulla got stuck in his throat during the wedding in Malianta village near Jamshedpur on Saturday. As his condition deteriorated, he was rushed to the city’s MGM Hospital but couldn’t be saved. “When food gets stuck in the windpipe (trachea) rather than the muscular food tube (oesophagus), it prevents oxygen from reaching the lungs, leading to death,” said Dr Mrityunjay Kumar of MGM Hospital.

End of Article

title: Veteran CB Slay retires to 'start my new journey'
content: veteran cornerback darius Slay announced his retirement from the NFL on monday . he was claimed by the Buffalo Bills on waivers last December . the 35-year-old played in 10 games for the Steelers last season .

title: Farooq attacker’s remand extended
content:
.

JAMMU: A Jammu court on Monday extended till March 23 the police remand of Kamal Singh Jamwal, a 63-year-old arrested for attempting to shoot National Conference (NC) president Farooq Abdullah at a Jammu wedding last week. An SIT produced Jamwal in court and argued that his continued remand was necessary for sustained interrogation about his motives to target the former J&K CM. The resident of Jammu’s Purani Mandi had said immediately after his arrest following the March 11 murder attempt that he had been planning for 20 years to assassinate Farooq. The court allowed the remand extension but told the police to ensure Jamwal’s medical examination every 24 hours. He has been charged under Section 109 of BNS (attempted murder) and Arms Act’s provisions. Jammu cops had initially ruled out any terror angle in the case.

End of Article

title: Soldier manning LoC dies of suspected cardiac arrest in J&K’s Poonch
content:
.

JAMMU: An Army jawan died of suspected cardiac arrest while on duty along the Line of Control (LoC) in J&K’s Poonch district on Sunday. The deceased soldier was identified as Naik Tilak Singh, who was posted in the forward Nangi Takri area in Krishna Ghati sector. He was found unconscious on duty along the LoC on March 15, and despite medical assistance, could not be revived, officials said Monday. Army’s White Knight Corps, in an X post, paid tributes to the soldier. “His unwavering courage and commitment to duty exemplify the finest traditions of the Indian Army. His supreme sacrifice will continue to inspire generations,” the post stated.

End of Article

title: Ultimate season preview: Predictions, inside intel and breakout stars for all 30 teams
content: the 2026 season is less than two weeks away, with the new york Yankees visiting the San Francisco Giants in a March 25 season opener followed by a full schedule of opening day games the next day . here is a team-by-team look at the year ahead for all 30 MLB teams .
